title: Retrieval Review: Flow, Positioning, and Stacking
summary: Retrieval Review: Flow, Positioning, and Stacking retrieves prior skills in an original changed content scroll and layer constraints scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css normal flow, css floats, css relative position, css absolute position, css fixed sticky, css stacking contexts in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real changed content scroll and layer constraints artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Volunteer Shift Deck positioning work here. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Continue the flow, containing-block, and layer system here. */

options.text: Changed content scroll and layer constraints case 1: professional layouts require positioning every major region explicitly.
Changed content scroll and layer constraints case 1: build a readable source-ordered flow baseline first and remove an element from flow only for a relationship that flow cannot express.
Changed content scroll and layer constraints case 1: absolutely position every heading card and action so browser flow cannot affect the design.
